2606.09466.title: DECSELFMASK: Leveraging Unlabeled Text via Self-Relevance-Guided Masking for Decoder-Only Classification
2606.09466.paper_summary: Classification tasks require annotated data, which can often be expensive, time-consuming, or even unfeasible to collect. This is the case of the medical domain, where large datasets often have few annotated examples. To address this, we propose DecSelfMask (Decoder Self-learning by Masking), an approach to enhance decoder-only performance on classification tasks. We build on common self-learning approaches by leveraging a model to create training examples from unlabeled data to propose a novel relevance-guided masking strategy. We use relevance attribution methods to determine what portions of unannotated texts are relevant for a task. We then create self-supervised training examples by masking out those portions, training the model to reconstruct them via next-token-prediction. We hypothesize that those examples convey knowledge about the structure and semantics of unannotated data that can be useful for downstream performance. We test our approach on 136 tasks from a collection of 1.9M clinical notes from an Italian hospital. We quantify DecSelfMask's impact on downstream tasks on 5 models of different scales and families, including a probing analysis. Experiments show consistent gains, outperforming standard supervised fine-tuning approaches (+19.9 points in Macro F1), synthetic label generation (+12.5), and continual pretraining (+6.3), as well as common baselines.
2606.09466.paper_summary_zh: 分類任務需要帶註釋的數據，這通常是昂貴的、耗時的，甚至是不可行的來收集。這在醫療領域尤為明顯，因為大型數據集中通常只有少量的帶註釋範例。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了DecSelfMask（解碼器自學習通過遮蔽），這是一種增強僅解碼器在分類任務上表現的方法。我們基於常見的自學習方法，利用模型從未標記數據中創建訓練範例，提出一種新穎的相關性引導遮蔽策略。我們使用相關性歸因方法來確定未標註文本中哪些部分與任務相關。然後，我們通過遮蔽這些部分來創建自監督的訓練範例，訓練模型通過下一個標記預測來重建它們。我們假設這些範例傳達了有關未標註數據的結構和語義的知識，這對下游性能是有用的。我們在來自意大利一家醫院的190萬臨床筆記的136個任務上測試我們的方法。我們量化了DecSelfMask對五個不同規模和家族模型的下游任務的影響，包括探測分析。實驗顯示出一致的增益，超越了標準的監督微調方法（+19.9點的宏觀F1）、合成標籤生成（+12.5）和持續預訓練（+6.3），以及常見的基線。
2606.09466.author: Pietro Ferrazzi et.al.
2606.09466.authors: Pietro Ferrazzi, Matteo Merler, Giovanni Bonetta, Alberto Lavelli, Bernardo Magnini
